title: misopates orontium subsp. orontium
intro: Misopates orontium subsp. orontium, also known as the golden club, is a perennial aquatic plant native to North America. It is characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers that bloom in the spring and early summer. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi. Golden club can be prepared as a tea or tincture to harness its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system.
